verses.text: The first covenant had its sanctuary, candlestick, table, and the ark with the mercy seat, 'of which we cannot now speak particularly.' Shadows. The earthly tent foreshadowed heavenly things.

The priests entered the first tent; into the second 'went the high priest alone once every year,' showing the way not yet open. Limited access. The old service could not perfect the conscience.

Christ, by 'his own blood, he entered in once into the holy place, having obtained eternal redemption for us.' Once for all. His one offering secured redemption forever.
Christ, by 'his own blood, he entered in once into the holy place, having obtained eternal redemption for us.' Once for all. His one offering secured redemption forever.

If the blood of bulls sanctified the flesh, 'how much more shall the blood of Christ... purge your conscience'? He is mediator of the new testament. Cleansed within. Christ's blood reaches the conscience itself.

If the blood of bulls sanctified the flesh, 'how much more shall the blood of Christ... purge your conscience'? He is mediator of the new testament. Cleansed within. Christ's blood reaches the conscience itself.
A testament is of force only after death; so even the first covenant was not dedicated without blood. Death required. A covenant of inheritance waits on the death of the testator.
A testament is of force only after death; so even the first covenant was not dedicated without blood. Death required. A covenant of inheritance waits on the death of the testator.
A testament is of force only after death; so even the first covenant was not dedicated without blood. Death required. A covenant of inheritance waits on the death of the testator.
Moses sprinkled the book and people with blood; 'without shedding of blood is no remission.' No bloodless pardon. Sin is forgiven only through a death.

Moses sprinkled the book and people with blood; 'without shedding of blood is no remission.' No bloodless pardon. Sin is forgiven only through a death.
The heavenly things needed better sacrifices; Christ entered 'into heaven itself, now to appear in the presence of God for us.' For us above. He pleads our cause before the Father.
The heavenly things needed better sacrifices; Christ entered 'into heaven itself, now to appear in the presence of God for us.' For us above. He pleads our cause before the Father.
He need not offer often; 'once... hath he appeared to put away sin by the sacrifice of himself.' One sacrifice. His single offering ended sin's claim.
He need not offer often; 'once... hath he appeared to put away sin by the sacrifice of himself.' One sacrifice. His single offering ended sin's claim.
'It is appointed unto men once to die, but after this the judgment'; so Christ, once offered, will 'appear the second time.' Death and judgment. He bore sin once and will return to save.
